validated_gaps.title: SLM-Specific Reasoning Evaluation Platform with Edge Deployment Metrics

demand_signals.signal: Ask HN thread 'Is anyone doing anything cool with tiny language models?' shows strong interest in edge/tiny model deployment with reasoning capabilities.
Demystifying Small Language Models for Edge Deployment paper documents the gap between reasoning quality and deployment constraints.
Edge intelligence survey identifies lack of modality-aware reasoning benchmarks and edge-oriented evaluation as key gaps.
Task-Specific Efficiency Analysis shows 0.5-3B models achieve superior Performance-Efficiency Ratios but no benchmark captures this.

validated_gaps.title: Contamination-Resistant Dynamic Reasoning Benchmark for SLMs

demand_signals.signal: BeyondBench accepted at ICLR 2026 shows academic recognition of contamination problem, but not SLM-calibrated.
'Line Goes Up?' paper documents inherent limitations of static benchmarks for LLM evaluation.
'Reasoning skills of LLMs are often overestimated' HN thread with community concern about benchmark contamination.

validated_gaps.title: Process Reward Model Evaluation Toolkit for Small Models

demand_signals.signal: Survey of Process Reward Models documents growing research interest but no SLM-specific toolkit.
Multiple papers on PRMs trending on HuggingFace, Mistral-7B shown as viable PRM.
Developer-facing explainer on PRMs indicates practitioner interest in making PRMs accessible.
Paper 'Why Do Multilingual Reasoning Gaps Emerge' documents 35% performance gap in 7B models between languages.
Scale AI launched MultiNRC benchmark, indicating commercial interest in multilingual reasoning evaluation.
MMLU-ProX multilingual benchmark shows growing demand for non-English evaluation.

validated_gaps.title: Continuous Reasoning Regression Monitoring for Production SLMs

demand_signals.signal: LangChain article 'LLM Evals: Production Monitoring to Regression Tests' documents the workflow gap between offline eval and production monitoring.
Top 5 LLM Monitoring Tools article shows growing market but no SLM reasoning specialization.
Evaluation platform review shows monitoring market growing but reasoning regression detection absent.

validated_gaps.title: Domain-Specific Reasoning Benchmark Generator for SLMs

demand_signals.signal: Meerkat medical SLMs paper shows 22% improvement with domain reasoning data, indicating domain-specific eval demand.
FLAME financial evaluation benchmark published, showing demand for vertical-specific eval.
HN thread on 'LLMs struggle in real mental health care' highlights domain evaluation gaps.
Red Hat ran 500K+ evaluations on quantized LLMs, found significant reasoning-specific degradation at lower bit widths.
LocalLLaMA community extensively discusses quantization impact on reasoning, with no standardized tool to measure it.
LessWrong post comparing quantized performance shows practitioner demand for systematic quantization reasoning eval.
Detailed analysis showing GSM8K reasoning degrades earlier than other tasks under quantization.

validated_gaps.title: Reasoning Chain Visualization and Debugging Tool for SLMs

demand_signals.signal: Large Language Model Reasoning Failures HN thread with 100+ comments shows developer frustration with opaque reasoning failures.
BeyondBench evaluated 101 models but provides no visualization of failure patterns across model sizes.

validated_gaps.title: SLM Reasoning Comparison Leaderboard with Efficiency Normalization

demand_signals.signal: Best Open-Source SLMs 2026 article attempts manual efficiency comparison but no automated leaderboard exists.
SLMs vs LLMs HN discussion shows developers want efficiency-normalized comparisons.
Artificial Analysis leaderboard tracks speed/cost but not reasoning-per-efficiency for SLMs specifically.

validated_gaps.title: Adversarial Reasoning Robustness Toolkit for SLMs

demand_signals.signal: ThinkSLM found 11-17% accuracy drops under adversarial perturbation, indicating need for robustness toolkit.
PromptBench's 33% performance drop on word-level attacks shows vulnerability, but no SLM-specific adversarial reasoning toolkit.
lm-eval-harness adding reasoning model support (think_end_token) shows framework-level demand but no CI/CD pipeline.
tinyBenchmarks' 140x cost reduction enables CI/CD-scale evaluation but no pre-built pipeline exists.
Stanford HELM integrating Unitxt shows recognition of standardization need but only covers data format, not scoring.
'30 LLM Benchmarks' article documents the fragmentation problem — different tools use incompatible metrics.
SLM-Bench tracks sustainability for SLMs but not reasoning quality, showing the gap between these two dimensions.

validated_gaps.title: Reasoning Evaluation for SLM Ensembles and Multi-Agent Systems

demand_signals.signal: SLM-MUX paper shows multi-agent SLM reasoning but uses ad-hoc evaluation.
LightReasoner shows SLMs can teach LLMs, indicating multi-model evaluation needs.

validated_gaps.title: Low-Cost / Self-Hosted Reasoning Evaluation (No LLM Judge Required)

demand_signals.signal: 'Thinking Small Models are Efficient LLM Judges' paper shows small models can replace expensive LLM judges.
CompassVerifier 3B model achieving >90% accuracy demonstrates viability of small self-hosted verifiers.
ThinkPRM using 1% of labels outperforms LLM-as-Judge, showing potential for low-cost verification.
